title: T-013 — Seed lp_state_machine_v1 DSL rule
prompt:
# T-013 — Seed lp_state_machine_v1 DSL rule

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §6.1 (workflow-as-data, ADR-029).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Seed the rule registry (02-SETTINGS §7) with `lp_state_machine_v1` defining the LP transitions and guards from PRD §6.1.

## Implementation contract
Add seed `packages/db/seed/rules/lp-state-machine-v1.json` containing the YAML/JSON DSL rule with:
- transitions table (from→to, guard, side_effects) per §6.1
- destructive flag for block/scrap transitions
- allowed_reasons per (from,to) pair
Provide `packages/db/seed/rules/lp-state-machine-v1.seed.ts` that upserts via the rule registry service.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/seed/rules/lp-state-machine-v1.json [create]
- packages/db/seed/rules/lp-state-machine-v1.seed.ts [create]
- packages/db/seed/rules/__tests__/lp-state-machine-v1.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the seed runs, when querying rule_registry, then `lp_state_machine_v1` v1.0 exists with transitions matching §6.1.
2. Given a guarded transition `available→reserved` is checked, when DSL evaluator runs, then it requires `wo.released AND hard_lock.available`.
3. Given a destructive transition (e.g. available→blocked), when DSL is queried, then `destructive=true` and reason_code is required.

## Test strategy
- RED: lp-state-machine-v1.test.ts asserts seeded rule structure and required-reason flags.
- Run `pnpm --filter db test lp-state-machine`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not deviate from PRD §6.1 transition table.
- Do not introduce client-side state machine (rule must be server-driven).
- Do not set transitions admins can edit at runtime — rule version locked v1.0.

pipeline_inputs.description: Seed lp_state_machine_v1 DSL rule per PRD §6.1 transitions and guards.
pipeline_inputs.details: JSON DSL + seed script. Rule version 1.0, server-driven, admin read-only. Destructive flag + allowed_reasons per (from,to) tuple.
acceptance_criteria: Given the seed runs, when rule_registry is queried, then lp_state_machine_v1 v1.0 exists with §6.1 transitions
Given the DSL evaluator checks available→reserved, when run, then it requires wo.released AND hard_lock.available
Given destructive transitions (e.g. available→blocked), when DSL is queried, then destructive=true and reason_code is required
test_strategy: RED: lp-state-machine-v1.test.ts asserts structure + destructive flags + required reasons
risk_red_lines: Do not allow admin runtime edits
Do not embed client-side state machine
